title: Valyrian.js guides
text: Valyrian.js guides Learn each public capability through its purpose, main APIs and place in an application flow. Every guide points to a working example when one is available and continues into the related API reference.

title: Valyrian.js recipes
text: Valyrian.js recipes Complete one focused task with a small set of public APIs. Each recipe links to the guide for the related Valyrian.js capability.

summary: Create a view, mount it, understand its lifecycle and control its updates.
text: Create a view, mount it, understand its lifecycle and control its updates. Before you start Choose the DOM target where `mount` will attach the root view. Outcome A mounted view with lifecycle cleanup and explicit update control. Milestones Completion check You can mount the root view, attach and clean up lifecycle work, control an update and unmount the application without leaving work active.
title: Your first application

summary: Connect screens, intercept navigation, load remote data and represent asynchronous work.
text: Connect screens, intercept navigation, load remote data and represent asynchronous work. Before you start Complete the first application path and identify the remote data source used by each screen. Outcome A routed application that loads remote data and exposes pending, success and failure states. Milestones Completion check You can navigate between screens, intercept a route change, load data and represent each asynchronous state without hiding failures.
title: Routing and data

summary: Choose a state primitive, organize shared values, run effects and validate user input.
text: Choose a state primitive, organize shared values, run effects and validate user input. Before you start Start with a mounted view and choose the Valyrian.js state primitive used by the flow. Outcome A form flow with an appropriate state primitive, explicit effects and schema validation. Milestones Completion check You can choose the state boundary, update shared values, clean up effects and reject invalid form input before submission.
title: State and forms

summary: Observe connectivity, retain work in a queue and use the service worker APIs exposed by Valyrian.js.
text: Observe connectivity, retain work in a queue and use the service worker APIs exposed by Valyrian.js. Before you start Start with a browser application and choose one asynchronous operation that can be retained for a later attempt. Outcome An application that reports connectivity, retains suitable work and manages its service worker lifecycle. Milestones Completion check You can react to a network change, distinguish queued from failed work and register or update the service worker through its public lifecycle.
title: Network, offline and PWA

summary: Render views on the server, isolate execution scopes and use server storage and transformations.
text: Render views on the server, isolate execution scopes and use server storage and transformations. Before you start Use an isolated Valyrian.js context for values that belong to one server request. Outcome A server-rendered view with request isolation, server storage and explicit output transformations. Milestones Completion check You can render a view on the server, keep request state isolated, use server storage and transform output without leaking work across requests.
title: Server rendering and execution

summary: Create a view with `v`, attach it with `mount` and remove the mounted root with `unmount`.
text: Create a view with `v`, attach it with `mount` and remove the mounted root with `unmount`. `v` creates the vnode to render. `mount` attaches the root view. `unmount` removes it and runs registered cleanup. Main APIs Limit `mount` requires a valid target. Use `unmount` to remove the mounted root. Related reference
title: Build your first Valyrian.js application

summary: Build a view from vnodes, attributes and child values. Use public vnode utilities when you need to inspect or transform supported vnode structures, and use directives when behavior belongs to the rendered node lifecycle.
text: Build a view from vnodes, attributes and child values. Use public vnode utilities when you need to inspect or transform supported vnode structures, and use directives when behavior belongs to the rendered node lifecycle. trust marks content for trusted rendering. Use it only with content whose origin and transformation you control. It does not validate or sanitize untrusted input. Main APIs Related reference
title: Compose views and control rendered content

summary: Use lifecycle hooks to attach work to the moments when a view enters, changes within or leaves the rendered tree.
text: Use lifecycle hooks to attach work to the moments when a view enters, changes within or leaves the rendered tree. Keep setup and cleanup paired so a removed view does not retain subscriptions or external resources. Main APIs Limit A hook should coordinate the lifecycle phase documented by its contract. Do not depend on undocumented ordering between unrelated views. Related reference
title: Run work through the view lifecycle

summary: Use update for a public runtime update, debouncedUpdate to group repeated requests and preventUpdate when an interaction must finish without the update that would normally follow it.
text: Use update for a public runtime update, debouncedUpdate to group repeated requests and preventUpdate when an interaction must finish without the update that would normally follow it. Use `update` immediately, `debouncedUpdate` for repeated requests and `preventUpdate` to suppress the current interaction update. Main APIs Related reference
title: Control when views update

summary: Create a Router from the routes your application exposes, then connect it to the application with mountRouter.
text: Create a Router from the routes your application exposes, then connect it to the application with mountRouter. The router resolves the current location and selects the matching route flow. Main APIs Error Handle unmatched or invalid navigation through the public router error flow. Do not assume that every location resolves successfully. Related reference
title: Connect views with the Valyrian.js router

summary: Use beforeRoute for work before a route completes, afterRoute for work tied to a completed transition and redirect when navigation should continue at another supported location.
text: Use beforeRoute for work before a route completes, afterRoute for work tied to a completed transition and redirect when navigation should continue at another supported location. `beforeRoute` and `afterRoute` run during route transitions. `RouterError` represents router failures. Main APIs Error A failed route transition enters the `RouterError` flow. Related reference
title: Control the route transition

summary: Use request to start an HTTP operation through the public client API.
text: Use request to start an HTTP operation through the public client API. `request` returns its successful result or enters its rejection path. Main APIs Error Rejected requests enter the error path of the calling flow. Related reference
title: Send requests with Valyrian.js

summary: Use Suspense around supported asynchronous content when the view needs a defined pending boundary.
text: Use Suspense around supported asynchronous content when the view needs a defined pending boundary. `Suspense` renders its fallback while asynchronous content resolves. Main APIs Error A pending boundary and a failed operation describe different states. Handle failures through the error path of the underlying operation. Related reference
title: Represent content that is still resolving

summary: Use Task when an interaction can start new work before earlier work finishes and only the current supported result should drive the application flow.
text: Use Task when an interaction can start new work before earlier work finishes and only the current supported result should drive the application flow. `Task` exposes the state of the current asynchronous run. Main APIs Limit Task coordinates work according to its contract. It does not make external side effects reversible. Related reference
title: Coordinate replaceable asynchronous work

summary: Use QueryClient as the shared coordinator for supported query and mutation flows.
text: Use QueryClient as the shared coordinator for supported query and mutation flows. Read QueryHandle and MutationHandle states from the application layer that renders progress, results and recovery actions. Main APIs Error Keep query and mutation failures distinguishable. A failed mutation may require a different recovery action from a failed read. Related reference
title: Coordinate server data with QueryClient

summary: `createPulse` returns operations to read, update and dispose one reactive value.
text: `createPulse` returns operations to read, update and dispose one reactive value. Read and change it through the operations exposed by the returned value. Main APIs Limit Each `createPulse` call creates one independent reactive value. Related reference
title: Share a reactive value with createPulse

summary: Use createPulseStore for the pulse-store contract, createMutableStore for the public mutable-store model and createEffect for work that reacts to supported state changes.
text: Use createPulseStore for the pulse-store contract, createMutableStore for the public mutable-store model and createEffect for work that reacts to supported state changes. `createPulseStore` and `createMutableStore` expose different state models. `createEffect` returns a disposer. Main APIs Limit The two store creators expose different contracts. Consult their reference entries before choosing by name alone. Related reference
title: Organize reactive state and its effects

summary: Use FluxStore when the application benefits from the action and state-transition model exposed by its public contract.
text: Use FluxStore when the application benefits from the action and state-transition model exposed by its public contract. Committed mutations update the `FluxStore` state rendered by the application. Main APIs Related reference
title: Organize state transitions with FluxStore

summary: Connect the state primitive that your application already uses with the matching public connector.
text: Connect the state primitive that your application already uses with the matching public connector. Follow the returned connection lifecycle documented by the API. Main APIs Error DevTools may be unavailable. The application must continue without an active inspection connection. Related reference
title: Inspect Valyrian.js state with DevTools

summary: Use FormStore to keep supported form values, errors and interaction state together. Use formSchemaShield for the schema validation path exposed by Valyrian.js.
text: Use FormStore to keep supported form values, errors and interaction state together. Use formSchemaShield for the schema validation path exposed by Valyrian.js. `FormStore` exposes values, validation errors, interaction state and submission state together. Main APIs Error Separate validation failures from submission failures because they require different responses. Related reference
title: Manage form values and validation

summary: Use NetworkManager to observe the network state exposed by Valyrian.js and NetworkEvent values to update the application flow.
text: Use NetworkManager to observe the network state exposed by Valyrian.js and NetworkEvent values to update the application flow. Connectivity is a signal, not proof that a remote operation will succeed. Main APIs Limit Keep request errors and network status as related but distinct states. Related reference
title: Respond to connectivity changes

summary: Use OfflineQueue when an operation can wait until the application can attempt it again.
text: Use OfflineQueue when an operation can wait until the application can attempt it again. `OfflineQueue` exposes queued, retried and failed operations. Main APIs Error A queued operation can still fail when retried. Preserve a recoverable failed state rather than presenting it as completed. Related reference
title: Retain supported work in an offline queue

summary: Use `registerSw` to register the service worker and `SwRuntimeManager` to read registration states.
text: Use `registerSw` to register the service worker and `SwRuntimeManager` to read registration states. `SwRuntimeManager` exposes registration progress, availability and failure states. Main APIs Related reference
title: Connect the application to its service worker

summary: Use the server render API to turn a supported Valyrian.js view into the output defined by its public contract.
text: Use the server render API to turn a supported Valyrian.js view into the output defined by its public contract. The `render` call receives the view and the inputs required by its public contract. Main APIs Related reference
title: Render Valyrian.js views on the server

summary: Use ServerStorage for values that belong to the supported server execution flow.
text: Use ServerStorage for values that belong to the supported server execution flow. `ServerStorage` creates, reads and updates values within the active server execution. Main APIs Related reference
title: Keep server values within their intended execution

summary: Use the public context APIs to create an execution boundary for state that must not leak into independent work.
text: Use the public context APIs to create an execution boundary for state that must not leak into independent work. Run related rendering and storage operations inside that boundary. Main APIs Limit Keep references created inside a scope within the lifetime documented for that scope. Related reference
title: Isolate Valyrian.js execution with scopes

summary: Use public server transformations to change supported output before delivery.
text: Use public server transformations to change supported output before delivery. Use inline, icon helpers and service worker resources only with the input and output types documented by their contracts. Main APIs Error Unsupported input enters the public error path of the transformation API. Related reference
title: Transform supported server output and resources

summary: Use the public translation utilities to resolve a message for the active locale and render it in the view that owns it.
text: Use the public translation utilities to resolve a message for the active locale and render it in the view that owns it. Keep message identifiers stable across supported locales. Main APIs Error When `t` cannot resolve a translation, it returns the documented fallback. Related reference
title: Translate application content

summary: Use the public money utilities with the value, currency and locale forms accepted by their contracts.
text: Use the public money utilities with the value, currency and locale forms accepted by their contracts. The money utilities preserve the numeric amount separately from its localized representation. Main APIs Error Formatting returns a localized representation and does not change the `Money` amount. Related reference
title: Represent money with the Valyrian.js utilities

summary: Use the native storage utilities to read, write and remove values through the public Valyrian.js contract.
text: Use the native storage utilities to read, write and remove values through the public Valyrian.js contract. Main APIs Related reference
title: Persist supported values in native storage

summary: Public validators report whether a value satisfies their configured rule.
text: Public validators report whether a value satisfies their configured rule. Use object-path utilities to access supported nested paths without duplicating traversal logic. Main APIs Limit Each validator checks only the rule represented by its API. Related reference
title: Validate values and access nested data

summary: Connect a root view with `mount`, then remove it with `unmount`.
text: Connect a root view with `mount`, then remove it with `unmount`. APIs used Starting point Define one root view that renders the application content. Steps Mount the view in `body`, then call `unmount` to remove the mounted root. Result The root renders, and `unmount` removes it and runs registered cleanup. Limits Call `unmount` after `mount` to remove the current root. Continue
title: Mount and unmount an application

summary: Pair lifecycle setup with cleanup when the rendered view is removed.
text: Pair lifecycle setup with cleanup when the rendered view is removed. APIs used Starting point Create a view that starts external work when it enters the rendered tree. Steps Register the timer with onCreate, return its cleanup, observe removal with onRemove and finish by unmounting the view. Result Removing the view clears the timer and runs the registered removal callback. Limits Every external resource started by a lifecycle callback needs matching cleanup. Continue
title: Clean up work when a view leaves

summary: Choose the public update path that matches one direct, repeated or prevented update.
text: Choose the public update path that matches one direct, repeated or prevented update. APIs used Starting point Define a search value and expose immediate and delayed update paths. Steps Prevent the input event update, request a debounced update after 150 milliseconds and keep a button for an immediate update. Result Typing groups repeated refreshes while the button requests the current view immediately. Limits `debouncedUpdate` schedules the latest requested update after the delay. Continue
title: Group repeated view updates

summary: Connect route resolution to the application root.
text: Connect route resolution to the application root. APIs used Starting point Create a router with a home route, one parameterized route and a 404 handler. Steps Register each route, read the user identifier from route parameters and mount the router in body. Result The router renders the matching heading or the not-found response for an unmatched location. Limits Keep route paths and fallback behavior explicit in the router definition. Continue
title: Mount a routed application

summary: Represent the pending, successful and failed outcomes of a request.
text: Represent the pending, successful and failed outcomes of a request. APIs used Starting point Create a request client for the API base path and represent pending, successful and failed states. Steps Mount the status view, request the profile, update the result in try or catch and refresh the view in finally. Result The page moves from loading to either the serialized profile or a recoverable failure message. Limits A rejected request enters `catch` before the view refreshes in `finally`. Continue
title: Load data from a view

summary: Give supported asynchronous content a defined pending state.
text: Give supported asynchronous content a defined pending state. APIs used Starting point Wrap profile content in a pending boundary with an explicit fallback. Steps Give Suspense a stable key, provide the loading view and mount the screen that owns the boundary. Result The screen has a defined loading state while the supported content resolves. Limits Handle operation failures separately from the pending fallback. Continue
title: Show a pending content boundary

summary: Prevent an older supported result from replacing the current task result.
text: Prevent an older supported result from replacing the current task result. APIs used Starting point Create a search task whose latest run supersedes earlier work. Steps Pass the task signal to fetch, start two searches and await the newest result before mounting it. Result The rendered result belongs to the latest search instead of the superseded request. Limits Cancellation coordinates the task result but does not reverse external effects. Continue
title: Keep the latest asynchronous task

summary: Read the public state of server-data operations from their handles.
text: Read the public state of server-data operations from their handles. APIs used Starting point Create one query for a todo list and one mutation that adds a todo. Steps Fetch the list, execute the mutation, invalidate the query after success and render both operation states. Result The mutation triggers query invalidation and the view reports separate query and mutation statuses. Limits Keep read failures and change failures distinguishable because they require different recovery actions. Continue
title: Coordinate a query and a mutation

summary: Update the application flow from the public network events.
text: Update the application flow from the public network events. APIs used Starting point Read the initial connectivity state and observe subsequent network changes. Steps Subscribe when the view is created, update the rendered status on each change and destroy the manager during cleanup. Result The view switches between Online and Offline as public network events arrive. Limits Connectivity does not guarantee that a remote operation will succeed. Continue
title: React to connectivity changes

summary: Retain a suitable operation and handle the result of a later attempt.
text: Retain a suitable operation and handle the result of a later attempt. APIs used Starting point Create an order queue with a network manager and an asynchronous delivery handler. Steps Subscribe to queue changes, enqueue one order, render pending and failed counts, then release the subscription, queue and network manager on removal. Result The view exposes queued and failed work while the queue attempts suitable operations later. Limits A queued operation may still fail. Never present it as completed until processing succeeds. Continue
title: Retry queued work after connectivity returns

summary: Keep form values, validation and submission outcomes distinguishable.
text: Keep form values, validation and submission outcomes distinguishable. APIs used Starting point Create a profile form with one required name field and an asynchronous submit handler. Steps Set the name, submit the form and render success only after the store reports a successful submission. Result The example submits Arya and renders either Profile saved or a prompt to check the form. Limits Keep validation failures separate from submission failures. Continue
title: Validate and submit a form

summary: `createPulse` returns a reader and a setter for one reactive value.
text: `createPulse` returns a reader and a setter for one reactive value. APIs used Starting point Create one reactive counter value with its reader and setter. Steps Increment the current value through the setter and render the value returned by the reader. Result The mounted view displays Count: 1. Limits This `createPulse` call exposes one reader and one setter. Continue
title: Share one reactive value

summary: Keep related values and effects in the supported store flow.
text: Keep related values and effects in the supported store flow. APIs used Starting point Create a reactive store with a named increment operation. Steps Change the state count through increment and render the resulting public store state. Result Calling increment with 2 produces a rendered count of 2. Limits The pulse store returns related values and named operations in one store. Continue
title: Organize structured reactive state

summary: Drive state changes through the public FluxStore contract.
text: Drive state changes through the public FluxStore contract. APIs used Starting point Create a Flux store with one named mutation. Steps Commit increment with an amount of 2 and render the resulting store state. Result The named transition changes the rendered count from 0 to 2. Limits `FluxStore` applies this change through the named `increment` mutation. Continue
title: Model named state transitions

summary: Connect the matching Valyrian.js state primitive without making inspection required at runtime.
text: Connect the matching Valyrian.js state primitive without making inspection required at runtime. APIs used Starting point Create a pulse and connect it to the available DevTools inspection flow. Steps Name the connection Count and render the reader returned by the connected pulse. Result The application renders the pulse value and can expose its changes to an available inspection environment. Limits When DevTools is unavailable, the connected pulse still returns its reader. Continue
title: Inspect state with DevTools

summary: Resolve visible content for the active locale.
text: Resolve visible content for the active locale. APIs used Starting point Register English and Spanish messages and select Spanish as the active language. Steps Set both dictionaries, activate es and render the greeting resolved by t. Result The mounted view displays Hola for the active language. Limits Keep message keys stable across supported languages. Continue
title: Translate a view

summary: Produce a supported locale-aware representation while keeping the underlying amount separate.
text: Produce a supported locale-aware representation while keeping the underlying amount separate. APIs used Starting point Parse a decimal subtotal, add five dollars in cents and format the total for US dollars. Steps Parse 19.90 with two decimal places, add Money.fromCents(500) and format the result with en-US and USD. Result The view renders the locale-aware representation of a 24.90 USD total. Limits Formatting represents a value. It does not validate a business transaction. Continue
title: Format a money value

summary: Read, write and remove a value through the public native storage contract.
text: Read, write and remove a value through the public native storage contract. APIs used Starting point Create a native store for application preferences. Steps Write the theme, read it, delete the stored key, render the captured value and clean up the store. Result The view displays the saved dark theme even though the key is removed before cleanup. Continue
title: Persist a local value

summary: Apply a public validator and access supported nested data.
text: Apply a public validator and access supported nested data. APIs used Starting point Select allowed fields, validate their values and read one nested profile value. Steps Pick role and pageSize, check the allowed role and finite number, then read profile.name with a fallback. Result Valid input renders Arya: editor. Unsupported roles and non-finite page sizes raise explicit type errors. Limits The validators check the selected role and finite-number rules. Continue
title: Validate data and read a nested path

summary: Creates the vnode description that Valyrian.js renders as part of a view.
text: v valyrian.js Creates the vnode description that Valyrian.js renders as part of a view. Import Public signature Parameters Result Related guide
title: v

summary: Connects a root view to a supported target and starts its runtime lifecycle.
text: mount valyrian.js Connects a root view to a supported target and starts its runtime lifecycle. Import Public signature Parameters Result Observable effects Connects the application root to the selected DOM target and starts an update. Related guide
title: mount

summary: Removes the mounted root and ends its mounted lifecycle.
text: unmount valyrian.js Removes the mounted root and ends its mounted lifecycle. Import Public signature Parameters It does not accept parameters. Result Observable effects Removes the mounted root and runs registered removal and cleanup callbacks. Related guide
title: unmount

summary: Requests a view update through the public runtime.
text: update valyrian.js Requests a view update through the public runtime. Import Public signature Parameters It does not accept parameters. Result Observable effects Updates the mounted root with the current component. Related guide
title: update

summary: Groups repeated update requests according to the timing behavior of the public API.
text: debouncedUpdate valyrian.js Groups repeated update requests according to the timing behavior of the public API. Import Public signature Parameters Result Observable effects Schedules the latest requested update after the delay. Related guide
title: debouncedUpdate

summary: Prevents the update that would normally follow a supported interaction.
text: preventUpdate valyrian.js Prevents the update that would normally follow a supported interaction. Import Public signature Parameters It does not accept parameters. Result Related guide
title: preventUpdate

summary: Registers work for the moment a view enters the rendered tree.
text: onCreate valyrian.js Registers work for the moment a view enters the rendered tree. Import Public signature Parameters Result Related guide
title: onCreate

summary: Registers work that runs after a view update.
text: onUpdate valyrian.js Registers work that runs after a view update. Import Public signature Parameters Result Related guide
title: onUpdate

summary: Registers cleanup work for the current view lifecycle.
text: onCleanup valyrian.js Registers cleanup work for the current view lifecycle. Import Public signature Parameters Result Related guide
title: onCleanup

summary: Registers work for the moment a view leaves the rendered tree.
text: onRemove valyrian.js Registers work for the moment a view leaves the rendered tree. Import Public signature Parameters Result Related guide
title: onRemove

summary: Registers a named directive for use on rendered nodes.
text: directive valyrian.js Registers a named directive for use on rendered nodes. Import Public signature Parameters Result Observable effects Registers or replaces the directive under its name. Related guide
title: directive

summary: Exposes the registered runtime directives by name.
text: directives valyrian.js Exposes the registered runtime directives by name. Import Public signature Parameters This symbol exposes a value and does not accept parameters. Result Related guide
title: directives

summary: Renders the supplied HTML string without validating or sanitizing it.
text: trust valyrian.js Renders the supplied HTML string without validating or sanitizing it. Import Public signature Parameters Result Observable effects Parses the supplied string through innerHTML without sanitizing it. Related guide
title: trust

summary: Represents a virtual node with its tag, properties, children and lifecycle state.
text: Vnode valyrian.js Represents a virtual node with its tag, properties, children and lifecycle state. Import Public signature Parameters Result Related guide
title: Vnode

summary: Creates a DOM or SVG element for the requested tag.
text: createElement valyrian.js Creates a DOM or SVG element for the requested tag. Import Public signature Parameters Result Related guide
title: createElement

summary: Exposes the vnode, component and event currently processed by the runtime.
text: current valyrian.js Exposes the vnode, component and event currently processed by the runtime. Import Public signature Parameters This symbol exposes a value and does not accept parameters. Result Related guide
title: current

summary: Identifies a fragment that groups children without adding a wrapper element.
text: fragment valyrian.js Identifies a fragment that groups children without adding a wrapper element. Import Public signature Parameters This symbol exposes a value and does not accept parameters. Result Related guide
title: fragment

summary: Builds a vnode representation from an existing DOM value.
text: hydrateDomToVnode valyrian.js Builds a vnode representation from an existing DOM value. Import Public signature Parameters Result Related guide
title: hydrateDomToVnode

summary: Checks whether a value satisfies a supported component shape.
text: isComponent valyrian.js Checks whether a value satisfies a supported component shape. Import Public signature Parameters Result Related guide
title: isComponent

summary: Reports whether Valyrian.js is running in a Node.js environment.
text: isNodeJs valyrian.js Reports whether Valyrian.js is running in a Node.js environment. Import Public signature Parameters This symbol exposes a value and does not accept parameters. Result Related guide
title: isNodeJs

summary: Checks whether a value is a plain-object component with a view.
text: isPOJOComponent valyrian.js Checks whether a value is a plain-object component with a view. Import Public signature Parameters Result Related guide
title: isPOJOComponent

summary: Checks whether a value is a Valyrian.js vnode.
text: isVnode valyrian.js Checks whether a value is a Valyrian.js vnode. Import Public signature Parameters Result Related guide
title: isVnode

summary: Checks whether a value is a vnode whose tag is a component.
text: isVnodeComponent valyrian.js Checks whether a value is a vnode whose tag is a component. Import Public signature Parameters Result Related guide
title: isVnodeComponent

summary: Exposes property names reserved by Valyrian.js.
text: reservedProps valyrian.js Exposes property names reserved by Valyrian.js. Import Public signature Parameters This symbol exposes a value and does not accept parameters. Result Related guide
title: reservedProps

summary: Applies one property value to the DOM represented by a vnode.
text: setAttribute valyrian.js Applies one property value to the DOM represented by a vnode. Import Public signature Parameters Result Related guide
title: setAttribute

summary: Marks a property name for special runtime handling.
text: setPropNameReserved valyrian.js Marks a property name for special runtime handling. Import Public signature Parameters Result Related guide
title: setPropNameReserved

summary: Reconciles the properties of a new vnode with its previous DOM state.
text: updateAttributes valyrian.js Reconciles the properties of a new vnode with its previous DOM state. Import Public signature Parameters Result Related guide
title: updateAttributes

summary: Updates the DOM rendered for a vnode and returns server output when produced.
text: updateVnode valyrian.js Updates the DOM rendered for a vnode and returns server output when produced. Import Public signature Parameters Result Related guide
title: updateVnode

summary: Coordinates route definitions and resolves navigation through the public router flow.
text: Router valyrian.js/router Coordinates route definitions and resolves navigation through the public router flow. Import Public signature Parameters Result Errors Router operations reject invalid middleware, invalid error conditions, circular error handling and subrouter misuse with RouterError. Related guide
title: Router

summary: Represents a router failure exposed for application-level handling.
text: RouterError valyrian.js/router Represents a router failure exposed for application-level handling. Import Public signature Parameters Result Related guide
title: RouterError

summary: Connects a router to the mounted application flow.
text: mountRouter valyrian.js/router Connects a router to the mounted application flow. Import Public signature Parameters Result Observable effects Mounts the router on the target and starts route resolution. Related guide
title: mountRouter

summary: Continues navigation at a different supported location.
text: redirect valyrian.js/router Continues navigation at a different supported location. Import Public signature Parameters Result Related guide
title: redirect

summary: Registers work that runs before a supported route transition completes.
text: beforeRoute valyrian.js/router Registers work that runs before a supported route transition completes. Import Public signature Parameters Result Errors Throws RouterError when the router is not mounted or the call occurs outside a component context. Related guide
title: beforeRoute

summary: Registers work associated with a completed route transition.
text: afterRoute valyrian.js/router Registers work associated with a completed route transition. Import Public signature Parameters Result Errors Throws RouterError when the router is not mounted or the call occurs outside a component context. Related guide
title: afterRoute

summary: Starts an HTTP operation through the public Valyrian.js client API.
text: request valyrian.js/request Starts an HTTP operation through the public Valyrian.js client API. Import Public signature Parameters Result Observable effects Starts an HTTP operation through the configured request client. Related guide
title: request

summary: Defines a pending boundary around supported asynchronous content.
text: Suspense valyrian.js/suspense Defines a pending boundary around supported asynchronous content. Import Public signature Parameters Result Errors Throws when rendered outside a component or without suspenseKey. Related guide
title: Suspense

summary: Coordinates asynchronous work whose current result may replace an earlier one.
text: Task valyrian.js/tasks Coordinates asynchronous work whose current result may replace an earlier one. Import Public signature Parameters Result Related guide
title: Task

summary: Coordinates the supported query and mutation flows shared by an application.
text: QueryClient valyrian.js/query Coordinates the supported query and mutation flows shared by an application. Import Public signature Parameters Result Related guide
title: QueryClient

summary: Exposes the public state and operations of a query.
text: QueryHandle valyrian.js/query Exposes the public state and operations of a query. Import Public signature Parameters Result Related guide
title: QueryHandle

summary: Exposes the public state and operations of a mutation.
text: MutationHandle valyrian.js/query Exposes the public state and operations of a mutation. Import Public signature Parameters Result Related guide
title: MutationHandle

summary: Exposes the network state and event flow supported by Valyrian.js.
text: NetworkManager valyrian.js/network Exposes the network state and event flow supported by Valyrian.js. Import Public signature Parameters Result Errors Throws NetworkError for an unavailable navigator, an unavailable connection or an invalid event type. Related guide
title: NetworkManager

summary: Identifies a public event in the supported network flow.
text: NetworkEvent valyrian.js/network Identifies a public event in the supported network flow. Import Public signature Parameters This symbol exposes a value and does not accept parameters. Result Related guide
title: NetworkEvent

summary: Represents a public network failure that the application can handle.
text: NetworkError valyrian.js/network Represents a public network failure that the application can handle. Import Public signature Parameters Result Related guide
